stories.title: Germany rebukes Trump over South Africa’s G20 ban
stories.summary: JOHANNESBURG, South Africa — German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul on Thursday criticized U.S. President Donald Trump’s announcement that he would exclude South Africa from the next G20 summit in Miami, saying it would be “in no one’s interest.” Speaking to students at the University of the Witwatersrand in Johannesburg, Wadephul described South Africa as “an indispensable member of the G20,” whose importance had been underlined by last year’s G20 summit in

stories.title: Canada pushes new UK government to join defense bank
stories.summary: LONDON — Canada has urged the new-look British government to join its defense bank, as it tries to deepen its European presence. In an interview with POLITICO, Canadian Minister of National Defence David McGuinty said he is “hopeful” Britain will now sign up to Ottawa’s Defence, Security and Resilience Bank after several of its backers were given key jobs under new Prime Minister Andy Burnham’s administration. McGuinty said Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney will raise the issue directly

stories.title: Pride organisers vow to march in Romanian city that has blocked event four years in a row
stories.summary: Oradea is only major city in EU where local authorities have repeatedly blocked Pride plans Campaigners in a Romanian city have vowed to go ahead with a Pride march after local authorities denied permission for the fourth consecutive year. Since 2023, campaigners in the western city of Oradea, which has a population of about 180,000, have sought permission to hold Pride events. However, each year they have faced opposition, making Oradea the only major city in the EU where local authorities have

stories.title: Czechia: Army helicopter crashes, killing at least one
stories.summary: A Czech army helicopter carrying five troops crashed in eastern Czech Republic, the military said.

stories.title: Hungary: Ex-PM Orban pledges resistance to Magyar 'tyranny'
stories.summary: Hungary's former prime minister alleges that his successor has "dismantled the rule of law and eliminated checks and balances." Peter Magyar has pursued Viktor Orban and his allies since winning April's elections.

stories.summary: Nagy port kavart egy ismert pszichiáter nyilvános Facebook-posztja, amiben egy budapesti kórházban szerzett negatív tapasztalatait osztotta meg. De vajon hogy lehet, hogy ugyanez a beteg egy másik kórházban rendes ellátást kapott? A nővérek nincsenek könnyű helyzetben, de a megfelelő kommunikáció nem csak pénzkérdés, sok múlik a helyi szervezeti kultúrán is.

stories.title: Olyan trógerül használják a budapestiek a szelektív gyűjtőszigeteket, hogy a kerületek sorra számolják fel őket
stories.summary: Sok köztéri gyűjtőnél ad hoc lomtalanítási pont alakul ki, de még az elvileg oda való szemetet sem képesek normálisan kidobni az emberek. Az illegális hulladéklerakás hatalmas suttyóság, de nem csak egyéni felelősség, a jelenlegi rendszerben viszont esély sincs a fejlődésre.

stories.title: Évekig ömlöttek a magyar milliárdok, a Tisza-kormány most felülvizsgálja az erdélyi focitámogatásokat
stories.summary: A magyar kormány egyelőre nem árulta el, milyen jövőt szán a határon túli futball támogatásának, de a Transtelex kérdésére először közölte a jelenlegi támogatási összegeket, és azt is megerősítette, hogy felülvizsgálják a programot. Tusványoson eközben a Sepsi OSK és az FK Csíkszereda vezetői már arról beszéltek, hogyan készülnek arra az esetre, ha kevesebb pénz érkezik Magyarországról. A Sepsi OSK és az FK Csíkszereda az elmúlt négy évben több mint 20 milliárd forintnyi magyarországi forrást kö

stories.title: Nyolc éve nem látta ilyen jónak a gazdasági helyzetet a magyar lakosság
stories.summary: A vállalatok és a lakosság várakozásait átlagoló mutató az orosz–ukrán háború kitörése óta nem állt ilyen jól.

stories.title: Hiába riogattak vele, nincs veszélyben Orbán pártelnöksége a mandátumkorlátozás miatt
stories.summary: Erre utalt pár napja egy posztban Répássy Róbert ügyvéd, a Fidesz egykori államtitkára, de nem pontosan idézte a párttörvényt.

stories.title: Az Orbán-kormány nélkül se ment egyszerűen, megegyeztek az új orosz szankciókról
stories.summary: A Politico és az Euractiv szerint többek között a bolgár, a görög, a francia, és az olasz kormány is puhított az eredeti javaslatokon.

stories.title: A Revolut is rááll a tranzakciós illetékre, mit tehetnek a vállalkozók?
stories.summary: Sokáig úgy tűnt, az üzleti ügyfeleket egyelőre elkerüli az a folyamat, amelynek keretében a Revolut a lakossági számlákat átterelte a magyarországi fióktelepéhez. Most viszont kiderült: a vállalkozói számláknál is jön a váltás, és ezzel együtt érkezik a pénzügyi tranzakciós illetéknek megfelelő díj is – igaz, egyelőre kedvezménnyel és csak később. A BiztosDöntés.hu szakértői megnézték, milyen átutalási költségekkel találkozhatnak a vállalkozások, egyéni vállalkozások.

stories.title: Ígéretes rák elleni injekció van a piacon, de a magyar betegek egyelőre nagyon nehezen férhetnek hozzá
stories.summary: Az amivantamab előrehaladott tüdőrák esetén segíthet. Olvasónk kiszolgáltatottságról és bizonytalanságról számolt be a gyógyszerengedélyek terén, sokan várnak az egyedi kérelmük elbírálására, miközben ez a feladat épp visszakerül a Batthyány-Strattmann László Alapítványtól az újjászervezés alatt álló egészségpénztárhoz.

stories.title: A szörnyeteg, aki nem hagyja magát eltörölni
stories.summary: Marilyn Mansont a botrányai miatt kivetette magából a szórakoztatóipar, de a Budapest Parkban adott koncertje alapján a saját mikrouniverzumában még mindig érinthetetlen. A koncert több volt sima nosztalgiázásnál, amin sokat dobott a Manson mögötti zenekar.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Orbán Viktorról: Nagyon nehezen tudom elképzelni, hogy a maffiafőnöknek nem merül fel a felelőssége egy maffiában
stories.summary: A NAV húsz évre visszamenőleg vizsgálhatná politikusok és családtagjaik vagyonosodását. Csökkentik az állami cégek vezetőinek fizetését, 3,8 millió forint lesz a plafon, amit a miniszterelnök mostani jövedelméhez igazítanak. Több feljelentést is tettek a korábban Szijjártó Péter felügyelete alá tartozó Eximbank által nyújtott százmilliárdos hitelek miatt.

stories.title: „Full lopás” – a forrásvesztés elkerülésére lecserélte a Gondosóra programot a kormány az EU helyreállítási alapjánál
stories.summary: Már az Integritás Hatóság vizsgálatának kezdete után, de a múlt heti feljelentés előtt kiszedték az Orbán-kormány digitális idősgondozási programját az uniós helyreállítási tervből.

stories.title: Závecz Research: 74 százalékon áll a Tisza támogatottsága a biztos választók körében
stories.summary: A Fidesz–KDNP-re csak 19 százalék szavazna, ha most lenne a választás. Az elmúlt 36 évben még soha nem fordult elő, hogy egy pártnak 4,5 milliós támogatottsága lett volna.

stories.title: Az osztrák élvonalba adta kölcsön magyar kapusát a Liverpool
stories.summary: Az előző szezonban Pécsi Ármin fizikumát fejlesztette a Liverpool, a Hartbergnél fokozatos meccsterhelést kaphat.

stories.title: Az osztrák tiniknek nagyon bejött, a briteknél kemény vitákhoz vezetett, de van köztes megoldás is
stories.summary: 16 lehet az új 18? Nem először merült fel, de most először valós esélye is van, hogy Magyarországon is levigyék a választói korhatárt. Nemzetközi tapasztalat már akad, bár Európában az országok többsége még csak vitázik a kérdésről. A korhatár csökkentése tudatosabb választókat nevel ki, és az elöregedő társadalmakban is fontos szerepe lehet.

stories.title: A rendőrség segítségével felállt a munkacsoport, ami Budapest közterületi problémáit kezelné
stories.summary: A Közterületi Koordinációs Munkacsoport fogja össze a közös munkát.

stories.title: Magyar Péter egyértelművé tette: olyan államfőjelöltet nem támogatnak, aki 2026 előtt szerepelt a politikában
stories.summary: Sok ajánlást kaptak. A Tisza-frakció több ülést is tarthat a jelöltről. Magyar szerint a politikán kívülről jött tiszás képviselők miatt is érdemes kicsit a megszokott kereteken kívül gondolkodni.

stories.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
stories.summary: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. I made my peace with this long ago – they don’t have to un

stories.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea

stories.title: EU fines Google €890m for competition breaches over search and apps
stories.summary: Firm told to treat third-party services that appear in its results in ‘fair and non-discriminatory manner’ Google has been fined a total of €890m (£760m) by the EU for breaches of online competition laws by its search and app store services. The European Commission, the EU’s executive arm, said Google had broken the Digital Markets Act by giving priority to its own services, such as shopping and hotel deals, in search results over those of its rivals. Continue reading...

The case, which had its first hearing on Monday, centers on a little-known US federal statute th

stories.title: Greek gas concession unlocks EU’s Russia sanctions package
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries reached a deal Thursday on a sweeping 21st sanctions package against Moscow, but only by allowing Greece to keep sending Russian gas to countries outside the bloc. The final sticking point — a proposed ban on shipping Russian gas outside the EU — was resolved through a limited exemption for contracts signed before Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, while prohibiting EU operators from entering into new ones, two EU diplomats said, speaking anonymously to discuss the

stories.title: France’s far left faces accusations of antisemitism from Olympics DJ
stories.summary: PARIS — Jean-Luc Mélenchon’s far-left France Unbowed party has come under renewed scrutiny over accusations of antisemitism after LGBT+ activist and feminist DJ Barbara Butch was forced to cut short a concert in Grenoble over the weekend. Butch, who is Jewish, plans to file a complaint against Mélenchon’s party for incitement to hatred and violence, her lawyer Audrey Msellati told Franceinfo radio Thursday. stories.title: R James Woolsey, embattled ex-CIA director under Bill Clinton, dies aged 84
stories.summary: Woolsey died of a stroke at Washington home after facing ‘Havana syndrome’ reported by some government officials R James Woolsey , a national security expert who led the CIA for two turbulent years after his appointment by Bill Clinton , has died. He was 84. Woolsey, who became CIA director in early 1993, died on Tuesday at his home in Washington, according to several newspapers. Conchita Sarnoff Woolsey, his wife, told the newspapers that he died from a stroke. Continue reading...

stories.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
stories.summary: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying, after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year. Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and busin

stories.title: Police admit investigation errors in run-up to Manchester synagogue attack
stories.summary: Greater Manchester chief constable says crucial data on phones seized from Heaton Park synagogue attacker was not examined Greater Manchester police have apologised for a “missed opportunity” to act before an attack on a synagogue. The force’s chief constable, Sir Stephen Watson, said the phones of the Heaton Park synagogue attacker, Jihad al-Shamie, were seized by officers but crucial data was not examined. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK households free to install plug-in balcony solar panels from end of August
stories.summary: Law change means renters and flat owners may be able to cut bills as energy costs soar amid Iran war British households will be able to buy plug-in solar panels later this summer after the government changed the law so the UK’s biggest retailers could bring them to market for the first time. People across the country will be able to join the millions across Europe generating their own electricity using “balcony solar panels” from 27 August, according to legislation passed last week. stories.title: British army forced to cancel training in Kenya after defence agreement dispute
stories.summary: Exercise for September called off after Kenya raises concerns over funding and jurisdiction for criminal offences The British army has been forced to cancel training for its troops in Kenya after the Kenyan government refused to ratify a bilateral defence agreement. While the UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement, Kenyan lawmakers had sought changes to give local authorities greater powers to hold British soldiers accountable for any offences committed in the country. stories.title: India: Cockroach party calls for nationwide protests on Friday
stories.summary: As youth protests spread to several Indian cities, the Cockroach Janta Party has formally called for nationwide protests on July 24 to demand the education minister's resignation. DW has more.

stories.title: Who is Ukraine's new military chief?
stories.summary: Mykhailo Drapatyi has been made commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces. His appointment follows a cabinet reshuffle that ousted defense minister Fedorov.

stories.title: US citizens are ramping up pressure on data centers
stories.summary: Rapid data center construction across the US, fueled by AI, comes with serious threats to the environment, public health and energy supply. Local opposition has blocked or delayed dozens of projects.

stories.summary: Germany is keen to boost its economy. On his third trip to Africa, Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul is visiting Nigeria and South Africa to promote greater cooperation.

stories.title: Trump threatens to bomb Tehran back for every Iranian attack in Hormuz

stories.title: Climate crisis-driven heat ‘sucking soils dry’ as extreme drought hits Europe
stories.summary: Analysis shows how heatwaves are making atmosphere thirstier, damaging water supplies, food production and power generation Extreme heat supercharged by the climate crisis is sucking Europe dry in summer and intensifying the drought hitting the continent, a scientific analysis has found. stories.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
stories.summary: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. stories.summary: The CJP got a boost on Tuesday when the opposition Congress party demonstrated outside the PM's house.

stories.title: Tens of thousands of Cockroach movement protesters defy police with Delhi march
stories.summary: Youth-led group calls on education minister to quit in one of India’s largest anti-government rallies in years Tens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years. A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already

stories.title: Drone strike sparks major fire in Sumy, northeastern Ukraine
stories.summary: A home improvement shopping centre in Sumy, Ukraine, catches fire after a reported Russian FPV drone strike as firefighters battle the blaze.

stories.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
stories.summary: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus. The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Sign up for US Breaking News email US gas prices have continued to rise since the conflict with Iran resumed this month. According to the American Automobile Association (AAA), a gallon of fuel is now just over $4 –  almost a dollar more than a year ago. It comes as Brent Crude , the international oil benchmark, is now up by almost 2% to $90.92 a barrel . Continue reading...

stories.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
stories.summary: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan c

stories.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
stories.summary: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode

stories.title: Scorched earth and a hungry wasp: photos of the day – Wednesday
stories.summary: The Guardian’s picture editors select photographs from around the world Continue reading...

stories.title: Two trams collide on bridge in Rotterdam, injuring at least 15 people - video
stories.summary: Webcam footage shows the moment two trams collide on the Erasmus Bridge in the Dutch city. Officials said at least 15 people were injured, one seriously, in the collision that occurred on Wednesday Continue reading...

stories.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
stories.summary: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections. The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alon

stories.title: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
stories.summary: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?

stories.title: 'Antifa' song climbs to top of charts after TV show cancellation
stories.summary: German rapper Danger Dan and pianist Igor Levitt had been invited to play a song for the 100th episode of a political satire show on TV. The performance was canceled at the last minute due to its message.

stories.title: Can VR help you achieve your goals?
stories.summary: A Max Planck researcher has developed VR and smartphone tools that let you "meet" your future self. The science behind it is more powerful than it sounds.

stories.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?
stories.summary: A year ago, the Kurdish militant group PKK announced an end to its armed struggle. Since then, peace talks with Turkey have progressed slowly, but some observers believe they could succeed.

stories.title: Police station opens in Hitler's birth house
stories.summary: Controversy has surrounded the renovation of the building in Braunau, Austria to this day. The debate points to something larger: the continued need to remember history and confront the Nazi past.

stories.title: Will surge in German business bankruptcies hit growth?
stories.summary: Company insolvencies in Germany are rising sharply. Is this a sign of deeper economic weakness, or just a market correction that could ultimately benefit future growth?

stories.title: Wildfire spreads in southeastern France as firefighters battle blaze
stories.summary: Firefighting aircrafts battled a fast-moving wildfire in southeastern France as the blaze spread across hundreds of hectares, prompting evacuations and threatening nearby communities.

stories.title: Firefighters battle major wildfire as thousands of hectares burn in central Spain
stories.summary: Firefighters battle a major wildfire in Spain's Guadalajara province as flames destroy thousands of hectares and force more than 1,200 people to flee.

stories.title: Russian prison intercepts cat used to smuggle drugs into penal colony
stories.summary: In Russia, offenders reportedly used a cat to smuggle illegal drugs into Correctional Colony No. 17 in the city of Nizhny Novgorod.

stories.title: US appeals court rejects Biden bid to block release of memoir ghostwriter recordings
stories.summary: A divided three-judge panel of the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit suspended its decision until 3 August to give Biden more time to consider a further appeal.

stories.title: Aliyev says former Russian and German officials were in Baku for possible meeting over Ukraine
stories.summary: Azerbaijan’s president revealed that former Russian and German officials were in Baku at the same time earlier in July, but that Azerbaijan’s authorities were neither notified nor aware of the meeting, which might have involved discussions over Ukraine.

stories.title: Trump says US has no interest in Iran talks as cost of war increases
stories.summary: The US entered its 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, while Tehran expanded its offensive against Gulf states. Meanwhile, Pakistan stepped up diplomatic efforts in hopes of salvaging the interim deal between the two.

stories.title: Outcry in Algeria over doctor deaths after gruelling on-call shifts
stories.summary: A resident neurosurgeon died of a heart attack after a 24-hour shift, and an anaesthesiologist died days later — deaths colleagues say reflect years of chronic overwork, understaffing and inadequate conditions in Algeria's public hospitals, demanding a revision of on-call rules dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Watch: Beluga whales travel by plane and lorry to US from closed Canadian marine park
stories.summary: Six beluga whales have arrived in Illinois and Texas after being moved from a Canadian marine park that could no longer care for them.

stories.title: Aerial footage reveals the scale of Spain’s La Mierla wildfire
stories.summary: Aerial footage shows the scale of the La Mierla wildfire in Guadalajara, Spain, as firefighters battle a blaze that has burned up to 30,000 hectares.

stories.title: Mamdani says NYC lacks authority to arrest Israeli PM Netanyahu
stories.summary: Netanyahu's office has dubbed the ICC a "kangaroo court" and called the arrest warrant against the Israeli leader "bogus."

stories.title: Zendaya's ancient Iranian earrings stir debate over stolen artefacts
stories.summary: Experts said the Iranian 3,000-year-old gold disc medallions Zendaya wore at a promo event for Christopher Nolan's Odyssey were "likely looted". Separately, a Zoroastrian symbol worn by IShowSpeed at the World Cup final has sparked further conversation about Persian cultural identity.

stories.title: 'It will get worse': World’s second-coldest capital faces record heat
stories.summary: Experts warn that the extreme heat reflects a broader climate trend that is increasing health, water and food security risks across Central Asia.

stories.title: Police station opens at Hitler's birthplace in bid to rid site of Nazi link
stories.summary: The opening follows years of controversy about what to do with the 17th-Century former inn, where the Nazi dictator was born.

stories.title: Drones hit Wildberries warehouses in Russia for second week running
stories.summary: Fires broke out at Wildberries warehouse sites in Krasnodar and Stavropol after overnight drone attacks, injuring 15 people and forcing both facilities to suspend operations.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hosts Azerbaijan President Aliyev
stories.summary: Talks between the German chancellor and Ilham Aliyev have focused on energy. Meanwhile, Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt will present a report on sexual offenses against children.

stories.title: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz as US strikes Iran for tenth consecutive night
stories.summary: As fighting escalates between both sides, Iran's interior minister travelled to Pakistan, a key mediator in the conflict, for talks.

stories.title: Iran's IRGC claims attack on Amazon's main data hub in Bahrain
stories.summary: The IRGC claimed its cruise missiles destroyed Amazon's central data infrastructure in Bahrain and hit US air defence systems. Bahraini, US and Amazon officials have not responded.

stories.title: Lebanese army begins deployment to southern regions after Israel deal
stories.summary: Beirut announced the beginning of its mobilisation to the southern Lebanese regions as President Aoun prepares for a high-stakes meeting with US President Donald Trump on Tuesday to conclude his four-day visit to Washington.

stories.title: UN reports 144 dead or missing after migrant boat is stranded at sea for 25 days
stories.summary: Two children onboard the boat bound for the Canary Islands lost their entire family before the passengers were picked up by a rescue operation from Mauritania.

stories.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
stories.summary: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the globa

stories.title: Rescuers in Afghanistan search for missing and dead after flash floods destroy homes
stories.summary: National disaster agency confirms ‘severe financial and human losses’ in eastern province of Nuristan Rescue crews in Afghanistan are searching for survivors and bodies among the rubble of houses destroyed by devastating flash floods that authorities said had killed nearly two dozen people and left more than 100 people missing. The Afghanistan national disaster management authority said on Tuesday that 23 people had died, 80 had been injured and more than 100 were missing after floods struck the

stories.title: What the EU wants from Andy Burnham
stories.summary: European leaders have made positive noises about the new UK PM, but he is likely to face the same restrictions as Starmer Andy Burnham is unknown in Brussels, but that will soon change. In his schedule for the autumn is an EU-UK summit that may underscore the same dilemmas that have confronted previous British prime ministers in the relationship with Europe. EU leaders were quick to offer congratulations to Britain’s prime minister, the seventh since the 2016 Brexit referendum. The European Comm
